# terrible_ieisystem_inmanage_edit_power_budget (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `action` (String) Type to action. Required when *range=False*.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `domain` (String) Domain id. Required when *range=False*.
- `end1` (Number) Pause period of add, end time, must be greater than start time, from 0 to 24.
- `end2` (Number) Pause period of add, end time, must be greater than start time, from 0 to 24.
- `end3` (Number) Pause period of add, end time, must be greater than start time, from 0 to 24.
- `end4` (Number) Pause period of add, end time, must be greater than start time, from 0 to 24.
- `end5` (Number) Pause period of add, end time, must be greater than start time, from 0 to 24.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `except_action` (Number) Except action, 0 is do nothing, 1 is send alert, 2 is shutdown system, 3 is shutdown system and send alert. Only the M7 model supports this parameter.
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `range` (Boolean) Range of power budget watts.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `start1` (Number) Pause period of add, start time, from 0 to 24.
- `start2` (Number) Pause period of add, start time, from 0 to 24.
- `start3` (Number) Pause period of add, start time, from 0 to 24.
- `start4` (Number) Pause period of add, start time, from 0 to 24.
- `start5` (Number) Period of add, start time, from 0 to 24.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `watts` (Number) Power budget watts of add. Required when *action=add*.
- `week1` (String) Pause period of add, repetition period. The input parameters are 'Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed', 'Thur', 'Fri', 'Sat', 'Sun', separated by commas, such as Mon, Wed, Fri.
- `week2` (String) Pause period of add, repetition period. The input parameters are 'Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed', 'Thur', 'Fri', 'Sat', 'Sun', separated by commas, such as Mon, Wed, Fri.
- `week3` (String) Pause period of add, repetition period. The input parameters are 'Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed', 'Thur', 'Fri', 'Sat', 'Sun', separated by commas, such as Mon, Wed, Fri.
- `week4` (String) Pause period of add, repetition period. The input parameters are 'Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed', 'Thur', 'Fri', 'Sat', 'Sun', separated by commas, such as Mon, Wed, Fri.
- `week5` (String) Pause period of add, repetition period. The input parameters are 'Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed', 'Thur', 'Fri', 'Sat', 'Sun', separated by commas, such as Mon, Wed, Fri.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `message` (String) Messages returned after module execution.
- `state` (String) Status after module execution.
